publisher.name: FinanceRateCalc
publisher.description: Independent lender-behavior intelligence from federal HMDA records. No ads, no lender compensation. Built by a 23-year banking professional.

license: Free to cite with attribution and a link. Cite as: FinanceRateCalc (FRC Intelligence), CFPB HMDA analysis.
dataset_basis: 310,592 FHA loan-level applications (CFPB HMDA 2018-2025), FHFA state HPI (1975-2026), Federal Reserve SLOOS, Freddie Mac PMMS.
disclaimer: Historical data observations. Denial rates partly reflect applicant mix. Overlays are lawful business decisions. Not financial, legal, or mortgage advice; not a recommendation to use or avoid any lender.

statistics.stat: In 2025, FHA denial rates across 11 major lenders ranged from 6.5% (CrossCountry, lowest) to 52.3% (NewRez, highest) — an ~8x spread on the same federal loan program.
statistics.key_fact: highest FHA denial rate 2025 by lender

statistics.stat: The lender with the HIGHEST 2025 FHA denial rate among major originators is NewRez at 52.3%. The lowest is CrossCountry at 6.5%.
statistics.key_fact: which lender denies the most FHA loans

statistics.stat: The deepest single denial cell in 2025 FHA records: Rocket Mortgage, DTI 48-50 x CLTV 90-95%, 97.6% denial rate (n=84).
FRC Denial Climate Index 2025: 54.3/100 (NEUTRAL). 2023 was the tightest credit year of the era at 66/100; 2021 the loosest at 21/100 — a 3x climate difference for an identical borrower.
statistics.key_fact: hardest year to get a mortgage approved

statistics.stat: At the median first-time-buyer down payment (10%, CLTV 90-94), the denial spread between major FHA lenders is ~79 percentage points (16.5% to 95.2%) — versus ~16 points at 20% down. Lower down payment makes lender choice ~5x more consequential.
statistics.key_fact: does down payment affect which lender to choose

statistics.stat: Lender DTI cliffs differ sharply: one major lender's denial rate jumps ~+15 percentage points across DTI 44-45, while another's moves only +1.4pp.
Texas home prices turned negative in 2026 (-0.1% YoY) for the first time in the FHFA state series, while California sits near-flat (+0.8%).
FHA denial rates predict FHA delinquency rates two years ahead (r=+0.618).
Unemployment correlates inversely with mortgage denial rates (r=-0.696) — the Recession Paradox.
Fed SLOOS survey responses show near-zero correlation with actual HMDA denial behavior (r=-0.150).
Rocket Mortgage's FHA denial rate jumps +54.9pp above 85% LTV; Mr. Cooper's jumps +1.7pp (2025 HMDA).
Identical high-risk FHA profiles face 26.7% denial at Mr. Cooper vs 91.2% at Rocket — a 64.5pp spread.
Shock Beta: pre-shock volatility predicted lender tightening response at r=+0.946; placebo easing episode confirmed at r=-0.605.
UWM's FHA denial rate rose from 16.2% (2022) to 21.8% (2025) — a 35% increase.
